2606.25358.title: Agentic Knowledge Tracing: A Multi-Agent LLM Architecture for Stealth Assessment of Financial Literacy in Serious Games
2606.25358.paper_summary: Assessing financial literacy during gameplay without disrupting the learning experience remains a key challenge in serious games for education. We present the Agentic BKT pipeline, a multi-agent large language model architecture for stealth assessment of financial competencies from open-ended gameplay events. The pipeline processes events from a 2D platformer serious game aligned with the OECD/INFE financial literacy framework through four phases: (1) the game captures every player decision as a structured event log; (2) an LLM event classifier labels each action on a four-point rubric validated against three domain experts (Fleiss kappa = 0.624, substantial agreement); (3) four domain-specific agents specializing in risk mitigation, investing, spending, and credit management perform session-level reasoning over behavioral trajectories, feeding per-competency Bayesian Knowledge Tracing that estimates mastery within each domain; and (4) an expert judge agent synthesizes the domain-level estimates into an overall mastery score. Evaluated with 193 K-12 participants across 264 game sessions, the Agentic BKT pipeline yields mastery estimates significantly correlated with learning gain (r = 0.276, p = 0.0001) and post-test scores (r = 0.333, p < 0.0001) while showing no correlation with pre-test scores, providing both convergent and discriminant validity. The multi-agent approach approximately triples the predictive validity of a single-LLM baseline (r = 0.095, not significant) in this study, demonstrating that domain decomposition and session-level reasoning play a central role in capturing the multidimensional nature of financial literacy from gameplay
2606.25358.paper_summary_zh: 評估遊戲過程中的財務素養而不干擾學習體驗，仍然是教育性嚴肅遊戲中的一個主要挑戰。我們提出了 Agentic BKT 管道，一種多代理大型語言模型架構，用於從開放式遊戲事件中隱秘評估財務能力。該管道通過四個階段處理來自與 OECD/INFE 財務素養框架對齊的 2D 平台遊戲的事件：(1) 遊戲將每個玩家的決策捕捉為結構化事件日誌；(2) LLM 事件分類器根據三位領域專家的驗證標記每個行動，使用四點評分標準（Fleiss kappa = 0.624，實質性一致）；(3) 四個專注於風險緩解、投資、消費和信用管理的領域特定代理在行為軌跡上進行會話級推理，提供每個能力的貝葉斯知識追蹤，估計每個領域的掌握情況；(4) 一個專家評判代理將領域級估計綜合成整體掌握分數。在 193 名 K-12 參與者和 264 次遊戲會話的評估中，Agentic BKT 管道產生的掌握估計與學習增益顯著相關（r = 0.276，p = 0.0001）以及後測分數（r = 0.333，p < 0.0001），而與前測分數無相關性，提供了收斂和區別的有效性。這種多代理方法在本研究中將單一 LLM 基準的預測有效性大約提高了三倍（r = 0.095，不顯著），顯示出領域分解和會話級推理在捕捉遊戲中財務素養的多維性方面發揮了核心作用。
2606.25358.author: Gabriel Santos et.al.
2606.25358.authors: Gabriel Santos, Rita Julia, Marcelo Nascimento
